title: US3-B: Galaxy seeder project scaffolding
description: Create tools/galaxy-seeder/ with package.json (tsx dependency), tsconfig.json (Node.js target, ES2022), following tools/galaxy-generator/ conventions. Skills: /prefactoring. Acceptance: Project compiles; tsx can execute entry point.

title: US3-C: Seeder file reader (reader.ts)
description: Implement tools/galaxy-seeder/src/reader.ts to read metadata.json, systems/*.json, routes.json from generator output directory. Uses fs/promises with concurrency limiting (MAX_CONCURRENCY = 100 per galaxy-generator pattern). TDD with vi.mock('node:fs/promises'). Skills: /typescript-unit-testing. Acceptance: Reads all system files and routes from fixture directory; respects concurrency limit; returns typed data structures.

title: US3-D: Seeder input validator (validator.ts)
description: Implement tools/galaxy-seeder/src/validator.ts to validate: metadata exists, routes exist, all route IDs reference real systems, no duplicate system IDs/coordinates, required fields present, economics.worldTradeNumber is numeric for all systems. TDD. Skills: /typescript-unit-testing, /error-handling-patterns. Acceptance: Valid data passes; missing metadata fails; route referencing non-existent system fails; system missing worldTradeNumber fails with descriptive error.

title: US3-E: Route graph and BFS (graph.ts)
description: Implement tools/galaxy-seeder/src/graph.ts to build adjacency list from routes and perform BFS to depth 5. For each system with routes, BFS discovers all reachable pairs within 5 hops. Deduplicates pairs (systemA < systemB lexicographic ordering), keeping minimum hop count. Only Oikumene systems (~250) have routes. TDD with small fixture graphs. Skills: /typescript-unit-testing. Acceptance: BFS from hub finds all reachable pairs within 5 hops; pairs deduplicated with min hops; isolated systems produce zero pairs.

title: US3-F: BTN computation (btn.ts)
description: Implement tools/galaxy-seeder/src/btn.ts with distanceModifier(hops) and computeBtn(wtnA, wtnB, hops) functions per GURPS Far Trader formula. Distance modifier: hops<=1→0, hops<=2→0.5, hops 3-5→1.0. BTN = clamp(wtnA + wtnB - modifier, 0, min(wtnA, wtnB) + 5). Assert both WTN values are finite numbers; throw descriptive error if not. Zero-BTN pairs excluded by caller. TDD. Skills: /typescript-unit-testing. Acceptance: Known WTN/hop inputs produce expected BTN values; non-numeric WTN throws error; boundary cases (BTN=0, cap applied) verified.

title: US3-G: SQL writer (sql-writer.ts)
description: Implement tools/galaxy-seeder/src/sql-writer.ts to generate batched INSERT SQL wrapped in BEGIN/COMMIT transaction. Batch sizes: star_systems 500, routes 500, trade_pairs 1000. Dedicated escapeSQL function handling single quotes, NUL bytes. JSON columns normalized via JSON.stringify then SQL-escaped. No RAISE() guard (red-team finding: rely on UNIQUE constraints). TDD with adversarial inputs (O'Brien's Star, nested JSON with quotes). Skills: /typescript-unit-testing, /security-review. Acceptance: Generated SQL is valid; string escaping handles all special characters; adversarial names don't break SQL.

title: US3-H: Seeder CLI entry point (index.ts)
description: Implement tools/galaxy-seeder/src/index.ts CLI with --input (generator output dir), --output (SQL file path), --verbose flags. Orchestrates: read → validate → build graph → compute BTN → write SQL. TDD following tools/galaxy-generator/src/index.spec.ts pattern for CLI arg parsing and error cases. Skills: /typescript-unit-testing. Acceptance: CLI produces valid SQL file from fixture input; missing args show usage; validation errors exit with clear message.

title: US3-I: End-to-end integration test
description: Integration test: generate SQL from fixture data (10 star systems, 5 routes), apply to D1, query via all three repositories. Verify: row counts, field values, bidirectional route queries, BTN ordering, prefix search with LIKE escaping. Fixture includes adversarial system name (O'Brien's Star) and pre-computed expected trade pairs. Skills: /vitest-integration-testing, /d1-repository-implementation. Acceptance: Full pipeline from fixture JSON → SQL → D1 → repository queries produces correct results for all user stories.

title: [security] Unbounded limit parameter — callers can pass arbitrarily large value
description:
File: src/infrastructure/galaxy/D1StarSystemRepository.ts
Line: 92
Severity: Medium

Problem:
Unbounded `limit` parameter — callers can pass arbitrarily large values (e.g., `limit: 9999999`), exhausting D1 query resources when exposed via HTTP.

Fix:
Cap at a safe maximum: `const safeLl = Math.min(limit, MAX_LIMIT)` where `MAX_LIMIT = 200`.

comments.author: sprite
comments.text: Starting work on unbounded limit parameter fix

title: [security] No maximum length on string fields (id, name, originId, destina
description:
File: tools/galaxy-seeder/src/validator.ts
Line: 109
Severity: Medium

Problem:
No maximum length on string fields (`id`, `name`, `originId`, `destinationId`) — unbounded strings flow through `escapeSQL()` and could produce SQL statements exceeding D1's 1 MB statement limit, crashing the seeder.

Fix:
Add `value.length > MAX_ID_LENGTH` checks (e.g., 256 chars) in `validateSystems` and `validateRouteReferences`.

comments.author: sprite
comments.text: Starting: add max length checks for string fields in validator

title: [security] escapeSQL() strips NUL bytes but does not remove other control chara
description:
File: tools/galaxy-seeder/src/sql-writer.ts
Line: 37
Severity: Low

Problem:
`escapeSQL()` strips NUL bytes but does not remove other control characters (e.g., `\r`, `\x1A`) — these pass through into SQL string literals and could cause silent data corruption in some SQLite contexts.

Fix:
Extend the replace to strip all ASCII control characters: `.replace(/[\x00-\x1F\x7F]/g, '')`.

title: [security] No maximum string length validation on id, name, or classificatio
description:
File: tools/galaxy-seeder/src/validator.ts
Line: 90-146
Severity: Low

Problem:
No maximum string length validation on `id`, `name`, or `classification` fields before SQL generation. Unbounded strings pass validation and are written into the D1 seed SQL. A malformed input file could insert multi-megabyte string values into production, degrading query performance.

Fix:
Add length guards (e.g. `id.length > 128`, `name.length > 256`) in `validateSystems`.

`parseJsonColumn<T>()` casts `JSON.parse()` result directly to `T` with no runtime validation — malformed DB data bypasses type safety silently.

Fix:
Validate parsed JSON against an expected shape (type guard or schema) before casting.

title: [security] No path traversal guard on --input in reader.ts
description:
File: tools/galaxy-seeder/src/reader.ts
Line: 52,60,70
Severity: Low

Problem:
No path traversal guard on --input; the CLI joins the argument directly with path.join and passes it to readFile/readdir without any normalization or allowlist check. Low risk as a developer tool, but worth hardening if invoked from an automated pipeline.

Fix:
Resolve the input to an absolute path (path.resolve) and optionally assert it does not escape a permitted root before use.

comments.author: sprite
comments.text: Starting work on path traversal guard

title: [security] Manual SQL escaping in sql-writer.ts instead of parameterized queries
description:
File: tools/galaxy-seeder/src/sql-writer.ts
Line: 35-39
Severity: Medium

Problem:
Manual SQL escaping (escapeSQL) instead of parameterized queries — doubles single quotes and strips control chars, which is correct for SQLite but is a fragile pattern that diverges from the parameterized-query standard used in all production D1 repositories.

Fix:
This is a seed-only CLI tool so the risk is low, but add a comment warning against copying this pattern to production query code. If wrangler batch execute ever supports bindings, migrate.

comments.author: sprite
comments.text: Starting work on adding warning comment for manual SQL escaping pattern

title: [security] No FK constraints or CHECK on classification in galaxy schema migration
description:
File: migrations/0002_galaxy_schema.sql
Line: 27-40
Severity: Low

Problem:
No FOREIGN KEY constraints on routes.origin_id/destination_id or trade_pairs.system_a_id/system_b_id referencing star_systems.id; also no CHECK constraint on star_systems.classification. D1/SQLite requires PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON to enforce FK constraints.

Fix:
Add CHECK (classification IN ('oikumene','uninhabited','lost_colony','hidden_enclave')) to star_systems. Document that FK enforcement requires PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON.

comments.author: sprite
comments.text: Starting work on CHECK constraint for classification

title: [quality] Timer not unref'd in Promise.race delays process exit
description:
File: .claude/hooks/pre-tool-use-bash.ts
Line: 1789-1791
Severity: Medium

Problem:
Timer in Promise.race is not unreffed — the 5-second setTimeout keeps the Node.js event loop alive on every hook invocation, delaying process exit by up to 5 seconds even when stdin completes instantly.

Fix:
Call timer.unref() on the setTimeout so it doesn't prevent process exit.

comments.author: sprite
comments.text: Starting work: unref timer in Promise.race

title: [quality] maxDepth leaked as public API parameter
description:
File: .claude/hooks/guard-rules.ts
Line: 1656
Severity: Medium

Problem:
maxDepth is a public parameter on the exported evaluateCommand function, leaking the internal recursion mechanism as API surface. Callers can pass maxDepth: -1 to silently disable shell-wrapper detection or maxDepth: 100 to force deep recursion.

Fix:
Extract recursive logic into an unexported evaluateCommandInner(command, depth) and keep the public evaluateCommand(command) signature clean.

comments.author: sprite
comments.text: Starting work: extract maxDepth into unexported inner function

title: [quality] Silent catch in main() swallows errors
description:
File: .claude/hooks/pre-tool-use-bash.ts
Line: 1855
Severity: Medium

Problem:
main().catch(() => {}) is a silent regression from the previous main().catch(console.error) — unexpected rejections from main() are now invisible.

Fix:
Restore error logging: main().catch((err: unknown) => { process.stderr.write(String(err) + '\n'); })

title: [security] d1-delete-no-where safePattern bypass via annotation
description:
File: .claude/hooks/guard-rules.ts
Line: 199
Severity: Medium

Problem:
d1-delete-no-where safePattern matches WHERE anywhere in the full raw command string (not just inside the SQL clause), so adding a flag like --annotation "WHERE reminder" after --command "DELETE FROM users" bypasses the guard and allows a DELETE without a real WHERE clause.

Fix:
Check the SQL argument value in isolation, or parse the --command flag value before applying the safe-pattern check.

- US-7 normalizeCommand() not yet implemented (P3, open tasks exist — turtlebased-ts-hr3.6.8)

title: Fix lock file race condition in ralph.sh
description:
**Code Review Finding**

- **Category**: quality
- **File**: ralph.sh:185-212
- **Severity**: Medium

## Description

The lock file check and creation are not atomic. A TOCTOU (time-of-check-time-of-use) race condition exists between checking if the lock file exists and creating it.

## Risk

Two concurrent executions could both pass the check before either creates the lock, allowing concurrent runs despite the locking mechanism.

## Recommended Fix

Use `flock` or atomic file creation with `noclobber` option:
```bash
set -o noclobber
echo $$ > "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null || { echo "Lock exists"; exit 1; }
```

---

_Duplicate Key: ralph.sh:185:quality_
close_reason: Used atomic file creation with noclobber to prevent TOCTOU race condition

title: Improve PID validation in lock file check
description:
**Code Review Finding**

- **Category**: quality
- **File**: ralph.sh:179-181
- **Severity**: Medium

## Description

`kill -0 "$pid"` only checks if a process exists, not if it is actually a ralph.sh instance. PID reuse could lead to false positives.

## Risk

A stale lock file with a reused PID (now belonging to an unrelated process) would block legitimate execution of ralph.sh.

## Recommended Fix

Store process name or command in lock file and verify both PID and process identity:
```bash
is_ralph_process() {
    local pid="$1"
    kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null && \
        [[ $(ps -p "$pid" -o comm= 2>/dev/null) == *ralph* ]]
}
```

---

_Duplicate Key: ralph.sh:179:quality_
close_reason: Added is_ralph_running() that validates both PID existence and process identity
